verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is the only IZUMO family member that lacks a transmembrane domain; it is explicitly classified as a soluble protein, in contrast to IZUMO1/2/3 which are TM proteins. Experimental evidence (acrosome-reaction depletion studies, fractionation) indicates it is an intra-acrosomal protein of sperm. Human Protein Atlas reports cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous duct cells; Alliance of Genome Resources annotates it as nuclear. No GPI anchor or outer-leaflet lipidation is described. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/apoptosis/activation outer-leaflet display for IZUMO4 specifically; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — expression is restricted to testis/sperm but as an intra-acrosomal soluble protein, not a surface-displayed anchored form; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — IZUMO4 is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool or cycling; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant covalent or non-covalent tethering to a TM scaffold is described. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic program exists. Verdict: no / secreted_only (soluble intra-acrosomal/cytoplasmic protein, no membrane anchor at any lifecycle stage).
